title: daphne oleoides var. hispanica
intro: Daphne oleoides var. hispanica, commonly known as Spanish laurel, is a type of flowering evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat infections, soothe wounds, and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Spanish laurel include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and even help to lower blood pressure. Spanish laurel is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used in the form of essential oil, which is extracted from its leaves and flowers.
